sections.title: Eat & Drink
sections.sub: Boat-up dinners, breakfast biscuits, and every tap worth pulling up a stool for.

spots.name: The Cabana
spots.blurb: Drive the boat right up to their dock, grab a drink at the bar, and let the afternoon do its thing. Lakeside casual, done right.
spots.tip: The Orange Crush is the move.
spots.maps: Cabana Lake Keowee, 1285 Doug Hollow Rd, Seneca, SC

spots.name: Brooklyn Style Pizza
spots.blurb: Brooklyn-style pies worth leaving the lake for — some of the best pizza in the area, full stop.
spots.tip: The wings are the sleeper hit: 10 juicy wings for under ten bucks.

spots.name: The Smokin' Pig
spots.blurb: The post-lake ritual.
spots.tip: Get the hash over rice and don't ask questions.

spots.name: Vangeli's Bistro
spots.blurb: White tablecloth on Ram Cat Alley. Where you go when it's an anniversary, not a Tuesday.

spots.name: Aunt Sue's Country Corner
spots.blurb: Scenic-drive ice cream since before you were born.
spots.maps: Aunt Sue's Country Corner, Pickens, SC

spots.name: California's Mexican Grill
spots.blurb: A food cart that kept leveling up — now with a patio, full bar, stage, and margaritas on draft. Locals, lake people, game-day crowds: a total vibe.
spots.tip: Weekly live music. It's near the lake but there's no public dock access — arrive by road.

spots.name: Keowee Brewing Co.
spots.blurb: The de facto clubhouse. Dog-friendly patio, rotating taps, everyone you know is already there.
spots.tip: Trivia Mondays, Music Bingo Wednesdays, burger & beer special Thursdays, live music Fri–Sat — and growlers to go for the dock.
spots.maps: Keowee Brewing Company, 401 E Main St, Seneca, SC

spots.name: Keowee Shores Grill
spots.blurb: Decks hanging over the lake, draft beer in hand. The best part: you can dock right there.
spots.tip: Tie up at the dock, order a draft, stay longer than you planned.

spots.name: Ram Cat Cellars
spots.blurb: Wine bar meets date night with a show: you sear your own steak on a hot plate at the table, sourced from the butcher shop upstairs.
spots.tip: Higher end, big fun. Book it for date night and come hungry.

spots.name: Sisters Cafe
spots.blurb: The breakfast gem of Salem. Great biscuits, great prices, zero pretense.
spots.tip: The biscuits. That's it. That's the tip.
spots.maps: Sisters Restaurant, 281 Stamp Creek Rd, Salem, SC

spots.name: Jocassee Valley Brewing
spots.blurb: Coffee in the morning, beers on tap the rest of the day — plus weekly food carts and live music.
spots.tip: Last stop before Lake Jocassee, or the first one after.
spots.maps: Jocassee Valley Brewing Company, Salem, SC

spots.name: Crave Kitchen
spots.blurb: The fancy one — a seasonal menu that actually changes with the seasons, and food worth dressing up (a little) for.

spots.name: Daydrinkers Coffee
spots.blurb: Third-wave coffee downtown. Grab a cup and wander, or post up with a laptop — half of Clemson does during the school year.
spots.tip: They rotate roasters, so the cup you loved last month has a new rival.
spots.maps: Daydrinkers, 307 E North 1st St, Seneca, SC

spots.name: Free Hand Coffee
spots.blurb: Pendleton's coffee stop — they brew Filigree Coffee out of Florida, and it is exceptional.

sections.title: Drink
sections.sub: Patios, pours, and one surprisingly legit vineyard in the foothills.

spots.name: Victoria Valley Vineyards
spots.blurb: A French-style château casually sitting in the Blue Ridge foothills. Golden hour hits different here.

spots.name: The Spot on the Alley
spots.blurb: Ram Cat Alley's living room. Live bands, cold beer, zero pretense.
spots.maps: The Spot on the Alley, Seneca, SC

sections.title: On the Water
sections.sub: Two lakes, absurdly clear water, and more coves than you'll ever finish exploring.

spots.name: Dive Lake Jocassee
spots.blurb: Some of the clearest freshwater in the Southeast. Dive it, paddle it, or just float and stare.
spots.maps: Devils Fork State Park, Salem, SC

spots.name: South Cove County Park
spots.blurb: Paddleboard launch, swim beach, campsites on the point. The easy-button lake day.
spots.tip: The deep ramp here stays usable even when the lake drops.
spots.maps: South Cove County Park, Seneca, SC

spots.name: Jocassee Waterfalls Run
spots.blurb: Boat-access-only waterfalls pouring straight into the lake. Yes, you can swim under them.

spots.name: Kayak & SUP Rentals
spots.blurb: No boat? No problem. Grab a board or kayak in town and pick a cove — asterisk resolved.

sections.title: Worth the Drive
sections.sub: When you need a break from the water. It happens. Rarely.

spots.name: Sassafras Mountain
spots.blurb: The highest point in South Carolina, with a 360° observation tower straddling the NC line.

spots.name: Stumphouse Tunnel + Issaqueena Falls
spots.blurb: An abandoned 1850s railroad tunnel and a waterfall with a legend attached. One parking lot, two adventures.

spots.name: Keowee-Toxaway State Park
spots.blurb: The Natural Bridge loop is the best short hike on the lake — granite views straight over the water.

spots.name: Whitewater Falls
spots.blurb: 411 feet of showing off, ten minutes past Bad Creek. Bring the good camera.
